briefdescription.para: Event generated when the buffer is filled with samples.
Event generated when one of the limits is reached.
Event generated when the calibration is complete.
Event generated when the next buffer for continuous conversion is requested.
Event generated when the first buffer is acquired by the peripheral and sampling can be started.
Event generated when all supplied buffers are filled with results.
SAADC driver event types.
SAADC driver event handler.

detaileddescription.para:
When operating in the advanced mode:
when the sampling is performed by the external timer, the external timer can be safely started on NRFX_SAADC_EVT_READY and stopped on NRFX_SAADC_EVT_FINISHED.
call the nrfx_saadc_buffer_set() on NRFX_SAADC_EVT_BUF_REQ to achieve the continuous conversion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an SAADC driver event. The event structure is allocated on the stack, so it is valid only within the context of the event handler.

briefdescription.para: Function for initializing the SAADC driver.

parameterdescription.para: Interrupt priority.
Initialization was successful.
The driver is already initialized.

briefdescription.para: Function for uninitializing the SAADC driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function stops all ongoing conversions and disables all channels.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the SAADC driver is initialized.

parameterdescription.para: Driver is already initialized.
Driver is not initialized.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the internal timer CC value from the interval in microseconds.

simplesect.para: The internal timer runs at 16 MHz, so to convert the interval in microseconds to the internal timer CC value, we can use the formula: interval_cc = interval_us * 16 MHz where 16 MHz is the frequency of the internal timer.
The maximum value for interval_cc is 2047, which corresponds to approximately 7816 Hz ~ 128us. The minimum value for interval_cc depends on the SoC.

parameterdescription.para: The interval in microseconds to be converted.

briefdescription.para: Function for configuring multiple SAADC channels.

simplesect.para: The values of the nrf_saadc_channel_config_t::burst fields in channel configurations are ignored. They will be overridden with the value suitable for the selected driver operation mode.
The desired mode (simple or advanced) must be set after the channels are configured.
This function overrides previous configuration done on any channel by nrfx_saadc_channels_config or nrfx_saadc_channel_config.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the array of channel configuration structures.
Number of channels to be configured.
Configuration was successful.
There is a conversion or calibration ongoing.
Attempt to configure the same channel more than once or attempt to configure an invalid analog pin.

briefdescription.para: Function for configuring single SAADC channel.

simplesect.para: The values of the nrf_saadc_channel_config_t::burst fields in channel configurations are ignored. They will be overridden with the value suitable for the selected driver operation mode.
This function overrides previous configuration done on specified channel by nrfx_saadc_channels_config or nrfx_saadc_channel_config.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the channel configuration structure.
Configuration was successful.
There is a conversion or calibration ongoing.
Attempt to configure an invalid analog pin.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the currently configured SAADC channels.
Function for deconfiguring the specified SAADC channels.

simplesect.para: Pins associated with the deconfigured channels will be released after next nrfx_saadc_simple_mode_set() or nrfx_saadc_advanced_mode_set() call.

parameterdescription.para: Bitmask of channels to be deconfigured.
Deconfiguration was successful.
There is a conversion or calibration ongoing.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint32_t channel_mask, nrf_saadc_resolution_t resolution, nrf_saadc_oversample_t oversampling, nrfx_saadc_event_handler_t event_handler)
memberdef.name: nrfx_saadc_simple_mode_set

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the SAADC driver in the simple mode.

detaileddescription.para: The simple mode allows obtaining a single sample from each requested channel. The conversion can be done in a blocking or non-blocking manner. Sampling is initiated by calling nrfx_saadc_mode_trigger() once.

parameterdescription.para: Bitmask of channels to be used in the simple mode.
Resolution configuration.
Oversampling configuration.
Event handler provided by the user. In case of providing NULL, the conversion will be performed in the blocking manner.
Initialization was successful.
There is a conversion or calibration ongoing.
Attempt to activate channel that is not configured.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint32_t channel_mask, nrf_saadc_resolution_t resolution, nrfx_saadc_adv_config_t const *p_config, nrfx_saadc_event_handler_t event_handler)
memberdef.name: nrfx_saadc_advanced_mode_set

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the SAADC driver in the advanced mode.

detaileddescription.para: The advanced mode allows performing double-buffered conversions of arbitrary length. The conversions can be done in a blocking or non-blocking manner. When performing conversions in the non-blocking manner and nrfx_saadc_adv_config_t::internal_timer_cc is set to 0, sampling needs to be done by triggering NRF_SAADC_TASK_SAMPLE externally (for example by using the TIMER and/or the PPI/DPPI). When performing conversions in the non-blocking manner and nrfx_saadc_adv_config_t::start_on_end is false, the NRF_SAADC_TASK_START needs to be triggered on NRF_SAADC_EVENT_END externally (for example by using the PPI/DPPI). Sampling is initiated by calling nrfx_saadc_mode_trigger(). In case of performing conversions in the blocking manner, nrfx_saadc_mode_trigger() may need to be called several times as each call sample each requested channel once.

simplesect.para: The internal timer can only be used when a single input channel is enabled.
The internal timer can only be used in the non-blocking mode.

parameterdescription.para: Bitmask of channels to be used in the advanced mode.
Resolution configuration.
Pointer to the structure with the advanced mode configuration.
Event handler provided by the user. In case of providing NULL, the conversion will be performed in the blocking manner.
Initialization was successful.
There is a conversion or calibration ongoing.
Attempt to activate channel that is not configured.

Attempt to activate either of the following:
internal timer in the blocking mode,
internal timer with multiple channels enabled,
oversampling without burst with multiple channels enabled.

briefdescription.para: Function for supplying the buffer to be used in the next part of the conversion.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer to be filled with conversion results.
Number of samples in the buffer.
Buffer was supplied successfully.
The provided buffer is not in the Data RAM region.
The provided buffer is not aligned to the number of activated channels or is too long for the EasyDMA to handle.
The driver is in the idle mode.
Both buffers for double-buffered conversions are already set.

briefdescription.para: Function for triggering the conversion in the configured mode.

parameterdescription.para: Operation finished successfully in the blocking manner or started successfully in the non-blocking manner.
The driver is performing the conversion in the advanced blocking mode. Call the function again to continue the conversion.
There is no buffer provided. Supply the buffer using nrfx_saadc_buffer_set() and try again.
There is an ongoing conversion or calibration being performed in the non-blocking manner or the driver is in the idle mode.

briefdescription.para: Function for aborting the ongoing and buffered conversions.

simplesect.para: Aborting blocking conversion or calibration from different context is not supported. Perform the operation in non-blocking manner instead.
NRFX_SAADC_EVT_DONE event will be generated if there is a conversion in progress. Event will contain number of words in the sample buffer.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the SAADC channel limits.

detaileddescription.para: When limits are enabled and the conversion result exceeds the defined bounds, the handler function is called with the corresponding event as parameter.

simplesect.para: Before the limits are set, the driver operation mode (simple or advanced) has to be configured. Only non-blocking conversions can be monitored.
Changing of the driver operation mode disables all configured limits.

parameterdescription.para: Channel index.
Limit low value to generate interrupt. Use INT16_MIN to disable interrupt generation.
Limit high value to generate interrupt. Use INT16_MAX to disable interrupt generation.
Requested channel limits were set.
Attempt to activate the limits on disabled channel.
Attempt to activate the limits for blocking conversions.
Attempt to activate the limits without configured mode.

briefdescription.para: Function for starting the SAADC offset calibration.

parameterdescription.para: Calibration event handler provided by the user. In case of providing NULL, the calibration will be performed in the blocking manner.
Calibration finished successfully in the blocking manner or started successfully in the non-blocking manner.
There is a conversion or calibration ongoing.

briefdescription.para: Auxiliary symbol specifying default value for the SAADC acquisition time.
Auxiliary symbol specifying default value for the SAADC conversion time.
Maximum value of the internal timer interval in microseconds.
Symbol specifying internal reference voltage.

memberdef.name: NRFX_SAADC_DEFAULT_CHANNEL_SE
memberdef.initializer: { \ .channel_config = \ { \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_CONFIG_RES, \ (.resistor_p = NRF_SAADC_RESISTOR_DISABLED, \ .resistor_n = NRF_SAADC_RESISTOR_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_GAIN, \ (.gain = NRF_SAADC_GAIN1,), \ ()) \ .reference = NRF_SAADC_REFERENCE_INTERNAL, \ .acq_time = NRFX_SAADC_DEFAULT_ACQTIME, \ .mode = NRF_SAADC_MODE_SINGLE_ENDED, \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_BURST, \ (.burst = NRF_SAADC_BURST_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_CHOPPING, \ (.chopping = NRF_SAADC_CHOPPING_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_HIGHSPEED, \ (.highspeed = NRF_SAADC_HIGHSPEED_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CONV_TIME, \ (.conv_time = NRFX_SAADC_DEFAULT_CONV_TIME,), \ ()) \ }, \ .pin_p = (nrfx_analog_input_t)_pin_p, \ .pin_n = NRFX_ANALOG_INPUT_DISABLED, \ .channel_index = _index, \ }

briefdescription.para: SAADC channel default configuration for the single-ended mode.

detaileddescription.para:
This configuration sets up single-ended SAADC channel with the following options:
resistor ladder disabled
gain: 1
reference voltage: internal
sample acquisition time: 10 us
burst disabled

parameterdescription.para: Positive input analog pin.
Channel index.

memberdef.name: NRFX_SAADC_DEFAULT_CHANNEL_DIFFERENTIAL
memberdef.initializer: { \ .channel_config = \ { \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_CONFIG_RES, \ (.resistor_p = NRF_SAADC_RESISTOR_DISABLED, \ .resistor_n = NRF_SAADC_RESISTOR_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_GAIN, \ (.gain = NRF_SAADC_GAIN1,), \ ()) \ .reference = NRF_SAADC_REFERENCE_INTERNAL, \ .acq_time = NRFX_SAADC_DEFAULT_ACQTIME, \ .mode = NRF_SAADC_MODE_DIFFERENTIAL, \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_BURST, \ (.burst = NRF_SAADC_BURST_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_CHOPPING, \ (.chopping = NRF_SAADC_CHOPPING_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CH_HIGHSPEED, \ (.highspeed = NRF_SAADC_HIGHSPEED_DISABLED,), \ ()) \ NRFX_COND_CODE_1(NRF_SAADC_HAS_CONV_TIME, \ (.conv_time = NRFX_SAADC_DEFAULT_CONV_TIME,), \ ()) \ }, \ .pin_p = (nrfx_analog_input_t)_pin_p, \ .pin_n = (nrfx_analog_input_t)_pin_n, \ .channel_index = _index, \ }

briefdescription.para: SAADC channel default configuration for the differential mode.

detaileddescription.para:
This configuration sets up differential SAADC channel with the following options:
resistor ladder disabled
gain: 1/6
reference voltage: internal
sample acquisition time: 10 us
burst disabled

parameterdescription.para: Positive input analog pin.
Negative input analog pin.
Channel index.

briefdescription.para: Macro for getting number of bytes needed to store specified number of SAADC samples.

parameterdescription.para: Number of samples.

simplesect.para: Number of bytes needed to store specified number of samples.

briefdescription.para: Macro for getting specified SAADC sample from the filled buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer filled with SAADC samples.
Sample index.

briefdescription.para: SAADC driver advanced mode default configuration.

detaileddescription.para:
This configuration sets up advanced mode of the SAADC driver with the following options:
oversampling disabled
burst disabled
internal sampling timer disabled
triggering of the START task on the END event disabled

parameterdescription.para: Positive input analog pin.
Negative input analog pin.
Channel index.

briefdescription.para: Successive Approximation Analog-to-Digital Converter (SAADC) peripheral driver.
